intro.title: Hello, I'm Canning Li
intro.desc:
B.S. in Tourism Management, Southeast University
M.S. in Sustainable Tourism, CUHK
Currently at CYTS Bohn, stationed at Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum

recent.title: Recent Work · First Two Months at the Museum

item1.title: Supplier Communication & Procurement
item1.desc: Connecting with venue operation suppliers, managing procurement processes and business negotiations to ensure timely delivery of project materials.

item2.title: Brand IP Program Development
item2.desc: Participating in venue brand IP strategy planning and development, building a differentiated museum identity and systematic brand framework.

item3.title: Douyin & RED Full Operation
item3.desc: Building the venue's social media presence from scratch, launching and operating Douyin and Xiaohongshu (RED) channels with content strategy and execution.

item4.title: Product Selection & Sales
item4.desc: Participating in cultural and creative product selection and sales strategy development, optimizing product mix and improving sales conversion.

project1.title: Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum
project1.desc:
Stationed at Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum project, responsible for venue operation planning, visitor experience design, and project coordination.

Key responsibilities:
1) Venue daily operation planning and optimization;
2) Visitor flow design and experience planning;
3) Cross-department coordination and project management;
4) Operational data collection and analysis.

project2.title: Destination Brand Strategy
project2.desc:
Providing brand positioning, communication strategy, and marketing solutions for tourism destinations.

Core services:
1) Destination brand positioning and differentiation;
2) Market research and visitor profiling;
3) Brand visual identity and communication planning;
4) Integrated online-offline marketing.

project3.title: Sustainable Tourism Planning
project3.desc:
Based on sustainable development principles, providing ecological assessment, community engagement design, and green operation solutions.

Work modules:
1) Tourism ecological impact assessment;
2) Community engagement and benefit-sharing design;
3) Green operation standards;
4) Sustainable tourism certification support.

event1.title: Southeast University · Tourism Management
event1.desc: Systematically studied tourism management, covering tourism planning, hospitality management, and marketing. Earned Bachelor's degree.

event2.title: CUHK · Sustainable Tourism
event2.desc: In-depth research on sustainable tourism theory and practice, focusing on ecological conservation, community development, and social responsibility. Earned Master's degree.

event3.title: CYTS Bohn · Shenzhen S& T Museum
event3.desc: Participating in the Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum project, responsible for venue operation planning and project coordination.
